checks.finding: Every AC identifier referenced by T-001 through T-008 resolves to AC-001 through AC-016 in acceptance-tests.md.
All eight tasks declare Blockers as None or comma-separated existing T-NNN identifiers; blockers_format_valid is true.
REQ-001 through REQ-017 each appear in at least one Requirements field in T-001 through T-008.
AC-001 through AC-016 are each referenced by at least one task Requirements field.
Every task T-001 through T-008 references at least one REQ-NNN in its Requirements field.
No task title contains test, spec, or verify, and no task scope is exclusively test-file work; tasks that plan acceptance fixtures also include implementation artifacts.
In attempt 2 round 1, every task T-001 through T-008 has Approval: Draft and Status: Planned, both valid for a subsequent attempt.
T-001 through T-003 pair medium with acceptance-first, T-004 through T-007 pair high with tdd, and T-008 pairs low with test-after; precheck also records workflow_match_precheck PASS.
Each AC identifier is claimed in only one task Requirements field; T-005 references REQ-016 without duplicating T-006's primary AC-015 claim.
Every blocker reference in tasks.md resolves to one of T-001 through T-008, matching the dependency-graph.json nodes array.
Blockers format passed, and dependency-graph.json edges form an acyclic order ending at prerequisite-free T-001 and T-004 without revisiting a node.

Each task T-001 through T-008 has one coherent concern: layer artifacts, cross-layer indexes, interview guidance, structure validation, implementation-review binding, task-review binding, approval governance, or release metadata.

Every task has inspectable Done When evidence through named TEST IDs, fixture results, exit codes, diagnostics, hashes, report paths, or verification-log directories; T-008 additionally names exact version and CHANGELOG outcomes.
traceability.md Task Mapping contains T-001 through T-008, and its requirement rows contain only REQ-001 through REQ-017, all defined in requirements.md.
